key_issues: C1 CRITICAL: collect-all-vs-short-circuit is never decided and the spec's own examples imply opposite behaviours (items 'first failing element' vs unspecified multi-property failure); a value violating two sibling keywords at once (e.g. {required:[name],properties:{age:{type:number}}} on {age:"x"}) has no pinned error count, so short-circuit and collect-all implementations both pass every test. Pin the policy + add a two-keyword-failure count assertion.

C2 CRITICAL: properties/required against a NON-object value is untested and will raise a Racket contract error (hash-ref/hash-has-key? on a non-hash) instead of returning validation-errors. Must test {type:object,properties:{name:...},required:[name]} against 42/"str"/list/(json-null), AND the no-`type` variant {properties:...,required:...} against 42 (legal schema, no type guard to catch it first).

C3 CRITICAL: items against a non-array value (and items/enum without a guarding `type`) is untested and will raise on map/iteration; also type+enum co-occurrence verdict/count unpinned. Test {type:array,items:{type:string}} on (hasheq 'a 1) -> validation-errors not raise.

C5 CRITICAL: format keyword on a non-string value and the unknown-format policy are under-specified. {format:email} (no type) on 42 has no pinned verdict; unknown formats like {format:ipv4} are routed to 'deferred policy' (line 38) but format is NOT in the deferred-keyword list (line 42), so Part 6 never covers them -> a real silent-mis-validation hole. Recognizer must also not run on non-strings (crash risk).

C6 CRITICAL: format reject cases are only the easy TS fixtures ('not-a-uri','invalid-email'); a permissive recognizer (e.g. regexp-match? @) passes them yet accepts '@','a@@b','example.com'. Require >=1 adversarial reject per format AND pin one concrete documented limitation per recognizer, else the 'limitations in module docs' ask is vacuous.

C4 PROCESS: deferred-keyword policy choice (ignore-with-warning vs reject) is allowed to slip to implementation time and no test proves the SAME policy is applied UNIFORMLY across all five keywords; also warning cardinality (once-at-compile vs per-validate) is contradictory between line 45 and the Part 6 capture. Commit the policy in module docs before authoring Part 6 and assert uniformity + warn-once-at-compile.

SUGGESTED: empty/degenerate schemas ({}, {type:object} w/o properties, {required:[name]} w/o properties), empty enum [], compound-enum equal?-membership, 42.0-under-integer (inexact-integer trap, mentioned but not an AC), unknown non-deferred keyword policy (multipleOf/$ref), annotation-keys-must-not-suppress-a-failure reject test, two-handles-from-one-provider independence, and the symbol-vs-string required/properties key boundary (string `required` entries vs symbol jsexpr keys) are all unpinned and each is a plausible silent bug.
